g.data-narration: This diagram explains six ways to categorize software projects for better project management.
g.text: Some Ways Of Categorizing Software Projects

g.data-narration: Categorizing software projects helps in planning, resource allocation, and selecting the right development methodology.
g.text: • Aids in planning and resource allocation

g.data-narration: There are six main ways to categorize software projects: by size, domain, methodology, complexity, team structure, and nature.
g.text: Six Ways to Categorize Software Projects

g.data-narration: Small projects take less than 6 months and involve 1-5 team members, like mobile apps and simple websites.
Medium projects take 6-12 months with 5-15 members. Large projects take 1-3 years with 15-50 team members.
Very large projects take 3-5+ years with over 50 members, including defense systems and air traffic control.
Based on application domain, software includes business software like accounting and CRM, and scientific software for research.
Embedded software runs inside hardware devices like firmware. Real-time software must respond within strict time constraints.
Web applications run in browsers like e-commerce sites. Mobile applications are designed specifically for mobile devices.
Based on methodology, Waterfall follows sequential phases with stable requirements, while Agile uses iterative development for evolving needs.
Spiral model combines iterative development with risk assessment. DevOps focuses on CI/CD and automation for modern applications.
g.text: Combines iterative dev plus risk analysis

g.data-narration: Projects are classified by complexity: Simple uses standard technology, Moderate involves some new tech, Complex uses cutting-edge solutions.
Projects can be in-house, outsourced to external contractors, or offshore with teams in different countries.
Based on nature, software includes system software like operating systems, application software like MS Office, and utility software like antivirus.
A Hospital Management System is Large-sized, Real-Time domain, uses Hybrid methodology, and has Complex technical requirements.
A mobile calculator app is Small-sized with Simple complexity. An e-commerce platform is Very Large with Complex requirements.
Remember: projects have multiple categorizations, categories determine methodology and QA approach, and Waterfall suits stable requirements while Agile suits evolving needs.
g.text: Projects can have MULTIPLE categorizations simultaneously
Category determines methodology, docs, and QA approach
Real-time is not equal to Embedded: timing vs hardware
Waterfall for stable, Agile for evolving requirements

g.data-narration: For exams: memorize size thresholds, remember projects belong to multiple categories, and practice scenario-based classification.
g.text: 1. Remember size thresholds: Small(less than 6mo), Medium(6-12mo), Large(1-3yr), Very Large(more than 3yr)
2. A project belongs to MULTIPLE categories - do not force single classification
3. Practice scenario classification: Given a project, identify all applicable categories
